The extradimensional creature attached to the

The GM determines the time and plane.
If the bag is pierced or torn, it is destroyed, and
anything contained within it is transported to a
random location on the Astral Plane.

equipment.name: Bag of Holding
desc: This bag has an interior space considerably larger than its outside dimensions: roughly 2 feet in diameter at the mouth and 4 feet deep. It can hold up to 500 pounds, not exceeding 64 cubic feet in volume. The bag weighs 15 pounds regardless of contents. Retrieving an item from the bag requires an action.

If the bag is overloaded, pierced, or torn, it ruptures and is destroyed, and its contents are scattered in the Astral Plane. If turned inside out, its contents spill out unharmed, but the bag must be put right before it can be used again. The bag holds enough air for breathing creatures inside for a number of minutes equal to 10 divided by the number of creatures (minimum 1 minute), after which they begin to suffocate.

Placing a bag of holding inside an extradimensional space (such as a portable hole or Heward's handy haversack) destroys both items and opens a gate to the Astral Plane. Creatures within 10 feet are drawn through to a random location in the Astral Plane. The gate then closes and cannot be reopened.

random. On each of your turns, you must move as

the weapon, your turn ends after you've used up all
your available movement. If the creature dies or can
no longer be seen or heard by you, the next nearest
creature that you can see or hear becomes your
new target.

equipment.name: Vorpal Sword
desc: You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. In addition, the weapon ignores Resistance to Slashing damage.

When you use this weapon to attack a creature that has at least one head and roll a 20 on the d20 for the attack roll, you cut off one of the creature's heads. The creature dies if it can't survive without the lost head.

A creature is immune to this effect if it has Immunity to Slashing damage, if it doesn't have or need a head, or if the GM decides that the creature is too big for its head to be cut off with this weapon. Such a creature instead takes an extra 30 Slashing damage from the hit. If the creature has Legendary Resistance, it can expend one daily use of that trait to avoid losing its head, taking the extra damage instead.

equipment.name: Yklwa
desc: Pronounced: YICK-ul-wah, it consists of a 3-foot wooden shaft with a steel or stone blade up to 18 inches long.

equipment.name: Armor of Gleaming
desc: Armor (any medium or heavy). This armor never gets dirty.

equipment.name: Bead of Refreshment
desc: This spongy, flavorless, gelatinous bead dissolves in liquid, transforming up to a pint of the liquid into fresh, cold drinking water. The bead has no effect on magical liquids or harmful substances such as poison.

equipment.name: Boots of False Tracks
desc: Only humanoids can wear these boots. While wearing the boots, you can choose to have them leave tracks like those of another kind of humanoid of your size.

equipment.name: Candle of the Deep
desc: The flame of this candle is not extinguished when immersed in water. It gives off light and heat like a normal candle.

equipment.name: Cast-Off Armor
desc: You can doff this armor as an action.

equipment.name: Charlatan’s Die
desc: Whenever you roll this six-sided die, you can control which number it rolls.

equipment.name: Cloak of Billowing
desc: While wearing this cloak, you can use a bonus action to make it billow dramatically.

equipment.name: Cloak of Many Fashions
desc: While wearing this cloak, you can use a bonus action to change the style, color, and apparent quality of the garment. The cloak’s weight doesn’t change. Regardless of its appearance, the cloak can’t be anything but a cloak. Although it can duplicate the appearance of other magic cloaks, it doesn’t gain their magical properties.

equipment.name: Clockwork Amulet
desc: When you make an attack roll while wearing the amulet, you can forgo rolling the d20 to get a 10 on the die. Once used, this property can’t be used again until the next dawn.

equipment.name: Clothes of Mending
desc: This elegant outfit of traveler’s clothes magically mends itself to counteract daily wear and tear. Pieces of the outfit that are destroyed can’t be repaired in this way.

equipment.name: Dark Shard Amulet
desc: While wearing it, you can use the amulet as a spellcasting focus for your warlock spells. You can also try to cast a cantrip you don’t know; the cantrip must be on the warlock spell list. Make a DC 10 Intelligence (Arcana) check; on a success you cast it, otherwise the action is wasted. Once you try, you can’t try again until you finish a long rest.

equipment.name: Dread Helm
desc: This fearsome steel helm makes your eyes glow red while you wear it.

equipment.name: Ear Horn of Hearing
desc: While held up to your ear, this horn suppresses the effects of the deafened condition on you, allowing you to hear normally.

equipment.name: Enduring Spellbook
desc: This spellbook, along with anything written on its pages, can’t be damaged by fire or immersion in water. In addition, the spellbook doesn’t deteriorate with age.

equipment.name: Ersatz Eye
desc: This artificial eye replaces a real one that was lost or removed. While the ersatz eye is embedded in your eye socket, it can’t be removed by anyone other than you, and you can see through the tiny orb as though it were a normal eye.

equipment.name: Hat of Vermin
desc: This hat has 3 charges. While holding the hat, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and speak a command word that summons your choice of a bat, frog, or rat. The creature appears in the hat and tries to get away from you as quickly as possible. It isn’t friendly, not hostile, and not under your control. It behaves as an ordinary creature of its kind and disappears after 1 hour or when it drops to 0 hit points. The hat regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

equipment.name: Hat of Wizardry
desc: This conical hat is adorned with gold crescent moons and stars. While wearing it, you can use the hat as a spellcasting focus for your wizard spells. You can also try to cast a cantrip you don’t know; the cantrip must be on the wizard spell list. Make a DC 10 Intelligence (Arcana) check; on a success you cast it, otherwise the action is wasted. Once you try, you can’t try again until you finish a long rest.

equipment.name: Heward’s Handy Spice Pouch
desc: While holding the pouch, you can use an action to expend 1 of its 10 charges, naming any nonmagical food seasoning (such as salt, pepper, saffron, or cilantro), and remove a pinch of the desired seasoning from the pouch. A pinch is enough to season a single meal. The pouch regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn.

equipment.name: Horn of Silent Alarm
desc: This horn has 4 charges. When you use an action to blow it, one creature of your choice can hear the horn’s blare, provided the creature is within 600 feet of the horn and not deafened. No other creature hears the sound. The horn regains 1d4 expended charges daily at dawn.

equipment.name: Instrument of Illusions
desc: While playing this musical instrument, you can create harmless, illusory visual effects within a 5-foot-radius sphere centered on the instrument. If you are a bard, the radius increases to 15 feet. Sample visuals include luminous musical notes, a spectral dancer, butterflies, gently falling snow, and so on. The magical effects have neither substance nor sound, and they are obviously illusory. The effects end when you stop playing.

equipment.name: Instrument of Scribing
desc: This musical instrument has 3 charges. While you are playing it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge from the instrument and write a magical message on a nonmagical object or surface that you can see within 30 feet of you. The message can be up to six words long and is written in a language you know. If you are a bard, you can write an additional seven words and choose to make the message glow faintly, allowing it to be seen in nonmagical darkness. Casting dispel magic on the message erases it. Otherwise, the message fades away after 24 hours. The instrument regains all expended charges daily at dawn.

equipment.name: Lock of Trickery
desc: This lock appears to be an ordinary lock (of the type described in the Player’s Handbook) and comes with a single key. The tumblers in this lock magically adjust to thwart burglars. Dexterity checks made to pick the lock have disadvantage.

equipment.name: Moon-Touched Sword
desc: In darkness, the unsheathed blade of this sword sheds moonlight, creating bright light in a 15-foot radius and dim light for an additional 15 feet.

equipment.name: Mystery Key
desc: A question mark is worked into the head of this key. The key has a 5 percent chance of unlocking any lock into which it is inserted. Once it unlocks something, the key disappears.

equipment.name: Orb of Direction
desc: While holding this orb, you can use an action to determine which way is north. This property functions only on the Material Plane.

equipment.name: Orb of Time
desc: While holding this orb, you can use an action to determine whether it is morning, afternoon, evening, or nighttime outside. This property functions only on the Material Plane.

equipment.name: Perfume of Bewitching
desc: This vial contains magical perfume, enough for one use. You can use an action to apply it to yourself, and its effects last 1 hour. For the duration, you have advantage on all Charisma checks directed at humanoids of challenge rating 1 or lower. Those subjected to the perfume’s effect are not aware that they’ve been influenced by magic.

equipment.name: Pipe of Smoke Monsters
desc: While smoking this pipe, you can use an action to exhale a puff of smoke that takes the form of a single creature, such as a dragon, a flumph, or a froghemoth. The form must be small enough to fit in a 1-foot cube and loses its shape after a few seconds, becoming an ordinary puff of smoke.

equipment.name: Pole of Angling
desc: While holding this 10-foot pole, you can speak a command word and transform it into a fishing pole with a hook, line, and reel. Speaking the command word again changes it back into a normal 10-foot pole.

equipment.name: Pole of Collapsing
desc: While holding this 10-foot pole, you can use an action to speak a command word and cause it to collapse into a 1-foot-long rod for ease of storage. The pole’s weight doesn’t change. You can use an action to speak a different command word and cause the rod to revert to a pole; however, the rod will elongate only as far as the surrounding space allows.

equipment.name: Pot of Awakening
desc: If you plant an ordinary shrub in this 10-pound clay pot and let it grow for 30 days, the shrub magically transforms into an awakened shrub at the end of that time. When the shrub awakens, its roots break the pot, destroying it. The awakened shrub is friendly toward you. Absent commands from you, it does nothing.

equipment.name: Rope of Mending
desc: You can cut this 50-foot coil of hempen rope into any number of smaller pieces, and then use an action to speak a command word and cause the pieces to knit back together. The pieces must be in contact with each other and not otherwise in use. A rope of mending is forever shortened if a section of it is lost or destroyed.

equipment.name: Ruby of the War Mage
desc: Etched with eldritch runes, this 1-inch-diameter ruby allows you to use a simple or martial weapon as a spellcasting focus for your spells. For this property to work, you must attach the ruby to the weapon by pressing the ruby against it for at least 10 minutes. Thereafter, the ruby can’t be removed unless you detach it as an action or the weapon is destroyed. Not even an antimagic field causes it to fall off. The ruby does fall off the weapon if your attunement to the ruby ends.

equipment.name: Shield of Expression
desc: The front of this shield is shaped in the likeness of a face. While bearing the shield, you can use a bonus action to alter the face’s expression.

equipment.name: Smoldering Armor
desc: Wisps of harmless, odorless smoke rise from this armor while it is worn.

equipment.name: Spell Scroll (cantrip)
desc: A spell scroll bears the words of a single spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class’s spell list, you can read the scroll and cast its spell without providing any material components. Otherwise, the scroll is unintelligible. The scroll is consumed when the casting is complete.

equipment.name: Spell Scroll (1st level)
desc: A spell scroll bears the words of a single spell, written in a mystical cipher. If the spell is on your class’s spell list, you can read the scroll and cast its spell without providing any material components. Otherwise, the scroll is unintelligible. The scroll is consumed when the casting is complete.

equipment.name: Staff of Adornment
desc: If you place an object weighing no more than 1 pound (such as a shard of crystal, an egg, or a stone) above the staff while holding it, the object floats an inch from the staff’s tip and remains there until it is removed or until the staff is no longer in your possession. The staff can have up to three such objects floating over its tip at any given time. While holding the staff, you can make one or more of the objects slowly spin or turn in place.

equipment.name: Staff of Birdcalls
desc: This wooden staff is decorated with bird carvings. It has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge from the staff and cause it to create one of the following sounds at random, audible out to a range of 60 feet: a finch’s chirp, a raven’s caw, a duck’s quack, a chicken’s cluck, a goose’s honk, a loon’s call, a turkey’s gobble, a seagull’s cry, an owl’s hoot, or an eagle’s shriek. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the staff explodes in a harmless cloud of bird feathers and is lost forever.

equipment.name: Staff of Flowers
desc: This wooden staff has 10 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 charge and cause a flower to sprout from the staff’s tip. Unless you choose a specific kind of flower, the staff creates a mild-scented daisy. The flower is harmless and nonmagical, and it grows or withers as a normal flower would. The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the staff turns to inert wood and can’t be used again.

equipment.name: Talking Doll
desc: While this stuffed doll is within 5 feet of you, you can spend a short rest telling it to say up to six phrases, each phrase no more than 6 words long, and set a condition under which the doll speaks each phrase. You can also replace old phrases with new ones. Whatever the condition, it must occur within 5 feet of the doll to make it speak.

equipment.name: Tankard of Sobriety
desc: This tankard has a stern face sculpted into one side. Any nonmagical alcoholic beverage poured into it is purified and rendered nonalcoholic.

equipment.name: Unbreakable Arrow
desc: This arrow can’t be broken, except when it is within an antimagic field.

equipment.name: Veteran’s Cane
desc: When you grasp this walking cane and use a bonus action to speak the command word, it transforms into an ordinary longsword and ceases to be magical.

equipment.name: Walloping Ammunition
desc: This ammunition packs a wallop. A creature hit by the ammunition must succeed on a DC 10 Strength saving throw or be knocked prone.

equipment.name: Wand of Conducting
desc: This wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges and create orchestral music by waving it around. The music can be heard out to a range of 60 feet and ends when you stop waving the wand. The wand regains all expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand’s last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, a sad tuba sound plays as the wand crumbles to dust and is destroyed.

equipment.name: Wand of Pyrotechnics
desc: This wand has 7 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges and create a harmless burst of multicolored light at a point you can see up to 60 feet away. The burst is accompanied by a crackling noise that can be heard up to 300 feet away. The light is as bright as a torch flame but lasts only a second. The wand regains 1d6 + 1 expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand’s last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand erupts in a harmless pyrotechnic display and is destroyed.

equipment.name: Wand of Scowls
desc: This wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges and target a humanoid you can see within 30 feet of you. The target must succeed on a DC 10 Charisma saving throw or be forced to scowl for 1 minute. The wand regains all expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand’s last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand transforms into a wand of smiles.

equipment.name: Wand of Smiles
desc: This wand has 3 charges. While holding it, you can use an action to expend 1 of its charges and target a humanoid you can see within 30 feet of you. The target must succeed on a DC 10 Charisma saving throw or be forced to smile for 1 minute. The wand regains all expended charges daily at dawn. If you expend the wand’s last charge, roll a d20. On a 1, the wand transforms into a wand of scowls.

equipment.name: Potion of Greater Healing
desc: When you drink this potion, you regain 4d4 + 4 Hit Points.

equipment.name: Potion of Superior Healing
desc: When you drink this potion, you regain 8d4 + 8 Hit Points.

equipment.name: Potion of Supreme Healing
desc: When you drink this potion, you regain 10d4 + 20 Hit Points.

equipment.name: Potion of Fire Breath
desc: After drinking this potion, you can use a Bonus Action to exhale fire at a target within 30 feet (Dexterity save DC 13).
On a failed save, the target takes 4d6 fire damage; on a success, half as much. The effect lasts for 1 hour or until three exhalations are used.

equipment.name: Potion of Swimming
desc: You gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed for 1 hour when you drink this potion.

equipment.name: Potion of Hill Giant Strength
desc: When you drink this potion, your Strength score becomes 21 for 1 hour.

equipment.name: Potion of Stone Giant Strength
desc: When you drink this potion, your Strength score becomes 23 for 1 hour.

equipment.name: Potion of Frost Giant Strength
desc: When you drink this potion, your Strength score becomes 23 for 1 hour.

equipment.name: Potion of Fire Giant Strength
desc: When you drink this potion, your Strength score becomes 25 for 1 hour.

equipment.name: Potion of Cloud Giant Strength
desc: When you drink this potion, your Strength score becomes 27 for 1 hour.

equipment.name: Potion of Storm Giant Strength
desc: When you drink this potion, your Strength score becomes 29 for 1 hour.

properties.name: Ammunition
desc: You can use a weapon with the ammunition property to make a ranged attack only if you have the right ammunition to fire it.
Drawing the ammunition is part of the attack (you need a free hand to load).

properties.name: Finesse
desc: When making an attack with this weapon, you can use your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls.

properties.name: Heavy
desc: Small creatures have disadvantage on attack rolls with heavy weapons.

properties.name: Light
desc: A light weapon is small and easy to handle, making it ideal for fighting with two weapons.

properties.name: Loading
desc: Because of the time required to load this weapon, you can fire only one piece of ammunition from it when you use an action, bonus action, or reaction to fire it, regardless of how many attacks you can normally make.

properties.name: Reach
desc: This weapon adds 5 feet to your reach when you attack with it, as well as when determining your reach for opportunity attacks with it.

properties.name: Special
desc: This weapon has a special rule described in its item description.

properties.name: Thrown
desc: If a weapon has the thrown property, you can throw it to make a ranged attack.
If it is a melee weapon, you use the same ability modifier you use for a melee attack with it.

properties.name: Two-Handed
desc: This weapon requires two hands when you attack with it.

properties.name: Versatile
desc: This weapon can be used with one or two hands.
If used with two hands, it uses the weapon’s versatile damage die.

properties.name: Monk Weapon
desc: This weapon counts as a monk weapon for features that reference monk weapons.

weapon_masteries.name: Cleave
desc: If you hit a creature with a melee attack roll using this weapon, you can make a melee attack roll with the weapon against a second creature within 5 feet of the first that is also within your reach. On a hit, the second creature takes the weapon’s damage, but don’t add your ability modifier to that damage unless that modifier is negative. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

weapon_masteries.name: Graze
desc: If your attack roll with this weapon misses a creature, you can deal damage to that creature equal to the ability modifier you used to make the attack roll. This damage is the same type dealt by the weapon, and the damage can be increased only by increasing the ability modifier.

weapon_masteries.name: Nick
desc: When you make the extra attack of the Light property, you can make it as part of the Attack action instead of as a Bonus Action. You can make this extra attack only once per turn.

weapon_masteries.name: Push
desc: If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can push the creature up to 10 feet straight away from yourself if it is Large or smaller.

weapon_masteries.name: Sap
desc: If you hit a creature with this weapon, that creature has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

weapon_masteries.name: Slow
desc: If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to it, you can reduce its Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. If the creature is hit more than once by weapons that have this property, the Speed reduction doesn’t exceed 10 feet.

weapon_masteries.name: Topple
desc: If you hit a creature with this weapon, you can force the creature to make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus the ability modifier used to make the attack roll and your Proficiency Bonus). On a failed save, the creature has the Prone condition.

weapon_masteries.name: Vex
desc: If you hit a creature with this weapon and deal damage to the creature, you have Advantage on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

notes: Attuning requires a Short Rest focused on only that item while in physical contact with it.
You can voluntarily end attunement with a Short Rest focused on the item unless the item is cursed.
